Items.Title: 使用 PostgreSQL 数据库启动一个新的 Django 项目 | Starting a new Django Project with PostgreSQL database
Items.Description:
• 选择 IDE
  • 安装点子
  • 下载 Python
  1. 对 Django 的基本了解。
  2. 如何使用 CLI 的基本知识。
  3. 对 Git 的基本了解。

让我们直接进入它！

首先，前往您的终端并使用mkdir命令创建一个新文件夹。 该文件夹将承载您正在处理的项目的所有工作。
然后cd进入这个文件夹来创建一个虚拟环境。

有许多可用的虚拟环境工具。

在虚拟环境中工作可确保您隔离 Python 安装和关联的 pip 包，允许您安装和管理自己的一组包，这些包独立于系统提供的或其他项目使用的包。 根据您选择在机器上安装的虚拟环境，创建虚拟环境的命令会有所不同。

对于这个用例，我们将使用virtualenv 。

激活虚拟环境以便在虚拟环境中工作：

 $ source virtual/bin/activate

您现在可以将 Django 安装到这个专用工作区中。

 # This command will install the most recent version of django. (virtual) $ pip install django

要安装特定版本的 django，请按如下方式指定（将 == 符号后面的数字替换为您要安装的版本）：

 (virtual) $ pip install django==2.2.11

为了使协作更容易并跟踪您当前在虚拟环境中的所有包（及其版本），请使用以下命令固定您的依赖项。 这将创建文件requirements.txt 。 您可以在安装更多外部包以更新依赖项列表时分别运行此命令。

 (virtual) $ pip freeze > requirements.txt

项目和应用

  • 项目 - 代表整个网站的包。 项目目录包含整个网站的设置。 一个项目可以有许多应用程序。 使用以下命令创建项目：
 (virtual) $ django-admin startproject <project-name>

您的文件夹结构将如下所示：

 example/ │ ├── project/ │ ├── __init__.py │ ├── asgi.py │ ├── settings.py │ ├── urls.py │ └── wsgi.py │ └── manage.py
  • 应用程序 - 实现特定功能的项目的子模块。 例如，一个网站可以有一个用于发布帖子的应用程序和另一个用于支付的应用程序。 使用以下命令创建一个 django 应用程序：
 (virtual) $ python manage.py startapp <app-name>

将添加一个新文件夹。 您的文件夹结构将如下所示：

 example/ │ ├── app/ │ │ │ ├── migrations/ │ │ └── __init__.py │ │ │ ├── __init__.py │ ├── admin.py │ ├── apps.py │ ├── models.py │ ├── tests.py │ └── views.py │ ├── project/ │ ├── __init__.py │ ├── asgi.py │ ├── settings.py │ ├── urls.py │ └── wsgi.py │ └── manage.py

在将 git 添加到您的项目之前，或者在您可以提交到目前为止所做的更改之前，有些文件是您不想跟踪的。
这些是您添加到 gitignore 的一些文件。 您可以添加或省略任何内容。 例如，我通常有一个用于“粗略工作”的.txt文件，我将其添加到 gitignore。

 virtual/ .env *.pyc db.sqlite3 migrations/ media/*

在 gitignore 中添加迁移文件夹的原因是为了尽量减少生产中的合并冲突和错误。

您的项目还包含您不想跟踪的敏感数据。 诸如您的 django 密钥或数据库密码之类的数据。 此信息存储在.env文件中，然后将其放入 gitignore 文件中。

与其他人合作时，创建一个.env.example文件，其中包含其他合作者可以用他们自己的值替换的示例数据，以在本地运行您的项目。 这样，没有人提交他们的环境凭据，并且您不必在每次拉取项目时更改值。

然后，您可以在project/settings.py中引用这些凭据，如下所示：

 from decouple import config, Csv #add this to the top MODE=config("MODE") SECRET_KEY = config('SECRET_KEY') DEBUG = config('DEBUG', cast=bool) ALLOWED_HOSTS = config('ALLOWED_HOSTS', cast=Csv())

对于更复杂的项目，您将需要更强大的数据库，例如 PostgreSQL。

某些操作系统可能预装了 potgres，或者您可能需要安装它

要检查您是否安装了 PostgreSQL，请运行which psql命令。

  • 如果未安装 Postgres，则似乎没有输出。 您只需准备好终端提示符即可接受另一个命令：
  • 如果安装了 Postgres，您将收到包含 Postgres 安装位置路径的响应：
 > which psql /usr/bin/psql

要支持 postgres 数据库，您需要安装psycopg2和其他两个库。 对project/settings.py进行以下更改

import dj_database_url INSTALLED_APPS = [ 'application', #new 'django.contrib.admin', ... ] # Database # https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/3.1/ref/settings/#databases if config('MODE')=="dev": DATABASES = { 'default': { 'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.postgresql_psycopg2', #changed database from sqlite to postgresql 'NAME': config('DB_NAME'), 'USER': config('DB_USER'), 'PASSWORD': config('DB_PASSWORD'), 'HOST': config('DB_HOST'), 'PORT': '', } } else: DATABASES = { 'default': dj_database_url.config( default=config('DATABASE_URL') ) } db_from_env = dj_database_url.config(conn_max_age=500) DATABASES['default'].update(db_from_env)

使用git init命令初始化版本控制。 然后添加并提交您的更改。

通过运行以下命令检查您的设置是否有效：

 (virtual) $ python manage.py runserver

每当您需要在浏览器上测试代码时，您都将使用此命令。 默认端口为127.0.0.1：8000

你应该在浏览器上看到这样的输出：

至此，你已经为你的 Django 网站搭建好了脚手架，你可以开始通过添加模型、视图和模板来实现你的想法了。

命令摘要

命令按执行顺序：

命令描述
设置虚拟环境
激活虚拟环境
在虚拟环境中安装 django
建立一个 Django 项目
设置一个 Django 应用程序
将数据库连接到python
初始化并提交到 git
在 127.0.0.1 上查看网站： 8000

结论

在本文中，我们介绍了使用 PostgreSQL 数据库启动新 Django 项目的步骤，以及用于 Django Web 开发的常用终端命令。

我希望你觉得这篇文章有帮助！



原文:
  • Choosing an IDE
  • Installing pip
  • Download Python
  1. Basic understanding of Django.
  2. Basic knowledge of how to use CLI.
  3. Basic understanding of Git.

Let's jump right into it!

First, head to your terminal and create a new folder using the mkdir command. This is the folder that will host all the work for the project you are working on.
Then cd into this folder to create a virtual environment.

1. Create a virtual environment

There are many virtual environmnet tools available.

Working within a virtual environment ensures you isolate Python installs and associated pip packages, allowing you to install and manage your own set of packages that are independent of those provided by the system or used by other projects. Depending on the virtual environment you chose to install on your machine, the command to create a virtual environment will vary.

For this use case, we will be using virtualenv.

virtual is the name of my virtual environment.

Activate the virtual environment so as to work within the virtual environment:

$ source virtual/bin/activate

2. Install Django

You can now install Django into this dedicated workspace.

# This command will install the most recent version of django.
(virtual) $ pip install django

To install a specific version of django, specify it as follows(replace the number after the == sign with the version you wish to install):

(virtual) $ pip install django==2.2.11

To make collaboration easier and keep track of all packages(and their versions) you have currently in your virtual environment, pin your dependencies using the following command. This will create the file requirements.txt. You can run this command severally as you install more external packages to update the list of dependencies.

(virtual) $ pip freeze > requirements.txt

3. Create django project & app

Django is organized in two major parts; project and app

  • Project - the package that represents the entire website. The project directory contains settings for the whole website. A project can have many apps. Create a project using the following command: 
(virtual) $ django-admin startproject <project-name>

Your folder structure will look like this:

example/
│
├── project/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── asgi.py
│   ├── settings.py
│   ├── urls.py
│   └── wsgi.py
│
└── manage.py
  • App - a sub-module of a project that implements a specific functionality. For example, a website can have an app for posts and another app for payment. Create a django app using the following command: 
(virtual) $ python manage.py startapp <app-name>

A new folder will be added. Your folder structure will look like this:

example/
│
├── app/
│   │
│   ├── migrations/
│   │   └── __init__.py
│   │
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── admin.py
│   ├── apps.py
│   ├── models.py
│   ├── tests.py
│   └── views.py
│
├── project/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── asgi.py
│   ├── settings.py
│   ├── urls.py
│   └── wsgi.py
│
└── manage.py

4. Create gitignore & .env files

Before adding git to your project, or before you can commit the changes you've made so far, there are some files you don't want tracked.
The .gitignore file tells git to not track these files or any changes you make to them.

example/
│
├── app/
│   │
│   ├── migrations/
│   │   └── __init__.py
│   │
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── admin.py
│   ├── apps.py
│   ├── models.py
│   ├── tests.py
│   └── views.py
│
├── .gitignore
├── .env
├── .env.example
|
├── project/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── asgi.py
│   ├── settings.py
│   ├── urls.py
│   └── wsgi.py
│
└── manage.py

These are some of files you add to gitignore. You can add or omit anything. For example, I usually have a .txt file that I use for 'rough work' which I add to gitignore.

virtual/
.env
*.pyc
db.sqlite3
migrations/
media/*

The reason for adding migrations folder in gitignore is to minimize merge conflicts and errors in production.

Your project also contains sensitive data that you do not want tracked. Data like, your django secret key or your database password. This information is stored in a .env file which is then put in the gitignore file.

When collaborating with others, create a .env.example file that contains example data that other collaborators can replace with their own values to run your project locally. This way, no one commits their environment credentials and you don't have to change the values each time you pull the project.

Contents of .env may look like this:

SECRET_KEY=generate-a-key
DEBUG=True
DB_NAME=db-name
DB_USER=username
DB_PASSWORD=your-password
DB_HOST=127.0.0.1
MODE=dev
ALLOWED_HOSTS=*
DISABLE_COLLECTSTATIC=1

You can then reference these credentials in project/settings.py as follows:

from decouple import config, Csv  #add this to the top



MODE=config("MODE")

SECRET_KEY = config('SECRET_KEY')

DEBUG = config('DEBUG', cast=bool)

ALLOWED_HOSTS = config('ALLOWED_HOSTS', cast=Csv())

5. Database and settings.py

The default database used by Django out of the box is SQLite. For more complex projects, you will require a more powerful database like PostgreSQL.

Some operating systems may come with potgres pre-installed, or you may need to install it

To check if you have PostgreSQL installed, run which psql command.

  • If Postgres is not installed, there appears to be no output. You just get the terminal prompt ready to accept another command:
  • If Postgres is installed, you'll get a response with the path to the location of the Postgres install: 
> which psql
/usr/bin/psql

To support postgres database, you need to install psycopg2 and two other libraries. psycopg2 is a database adapter that connects databases to python.

pip install psycopg2
pip install dj-database-url
pip install python-decouple

Make the following changes to project/settings.py

import dj_database_url


INSTALLED_APPS = [
    'application',  #new
    'django.contrib.admin',
    ...
]



# Database
# https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/3.1/ref/settings/#databases
if config('MODE')=="dev":
    DATABASES = {
        'default': {
            'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.postgresql_psycopg2', #changed database from sqlite to postgresql
            'NAME': config('DB_NAME'),
            'USER': config('DB_USER'),
            'PASSWORD': config('DB_PASSWORD'),
            'HOST': config('DB_HOST'),
            'PORT': '',
        }
    }
else:
   DATABASES = {
       'default': dj_database_url.config(
           default=config('DATABASE_URL')
       )
   }

db_from_env = dj_database_url.config(conn_max_age=500)
DATABASES['default'].update(db_from_env)

6. Version tracking using git

Initialize version control using the git init command. Then add and commit your changes.

7. Test

Check that your set up worked by running this command:

(virtual) $ python manage.py runserver

You will use this command anytime you need to test your code on the browser. The default port is 127.0.0.1:8000

You should see an output like this on your browser:

At this point, you’ve finished setting up the scaffolding for your Django website, and you can start implementing your ideas by adding models, views and templates.

Summary of Commands

Commands in order of execution:

Command Description
$ virtualenv virtual setup virtual environment
$ source env/bin/activate activate the virtual environment
(virtual) $ pip install django Instal django inside virtual environment
(virtual) $ django-admin startproject <projectname> set up a Django project
(virtual) $ python manage.py startapp <appname> set up a Django app
(virtual) $ pip install psycopg2 connect database to python
(virtual) $ pip install dj-database-url
(virtual) $ pip install python-decouple
(virtual) $ pip freeze > requirements.txt pin dependancies and versions
Initialize and commit to git
(virtual) $ python manage.py runserver view website on 127.0.0.1:8000

Conclusion

In this article, we went through the steps of starting a new Django project with PostgreSQL database, as well as the common terminal commands used for Django web development.

I hope you found this article helpful!

Items.Description:
所以大约 4 年，我发现自己需要为在会议上发言而写传记。 我联系了dev.to寻求想法。

从那以后，我又在几次会议上发表了讲话，并且发现自己一直在调整那个生物。 就像谈话描述一样，总感觉还有改进的余地。 你改变。 你的谈话改变了。 为什么不确保这反映在您的 BIOS 中？

所以这是我开始使用的示例格式（取自对 dev.to 的评论）

他/她拥有 {#} 年的 {Technology Name} 经验，专注于 {Technology Focus}。 在他/她的业余时间，{Name} 喜欢 {Personal Interest #1} 和 {Personal Interest #2}。

瑞安

这确实让我得到了我的第一个简历：

她拥有 12 年的 Java 软件工程师经验，专注于 API 和微服务，并于今年 5 月涉足 Ruby。 在业余时间，她是一个狂热的长篇阅读者，热爱所有真实犯罪的播客，并正在尝试完成主要的阿斯汤加瑜伽系列。

现在我的简历看起来有点像这样：

她专业地帮助解决 WP Engine 身份团队的问题。 她喜欢与 Ruby 合作，向人们展示长期的 Git 提交令人印象深刻，并帮助编写安全、易于阅读的软件来支持她公司的授权和身份验证。

从那时起有什么变化？ 嗯，2018年我所在的公司被收购了。 我已经在团队中移动了 3-4 次，并且参加了更多的会议。

对我来说，你想从简历中得到的是，这个人是谁，我为什么要听他们的？ 看到那个人的个性也不会错。 我喜欢奇思妙想和积极向上，并且有很多其他的兴趣与我为什么要为我的职业生涯无关。 我的事业不是我的生活。 我希望我的简历能证明这一点。

所以让我介绍一个更新的会议简历模板：

在 {company} 任教、编码、设计、架构师、构建…} {tech stack}，对{技术/专业焦点}充满热情，并已在该领域工作了 {#} 年。 我谈论的是 MFA，所以我提到这就是我的职业生涯。}

你怎么看？ 这是否让您了解您想了解的有关会议发言人的信息？

原文:

So about 4 years, I found myself in need of writing a biography for speaking at a conference. I reached out to dev.to for ideas.

Since then, I have spoken at a couple more conference and have found myself tweaking that bio along the way. Just like talk descriptions, it always feels like there is room for improvement. You change. Your talk changes. Why shouldn’t you make sure that is reflected in your bios?

So here was a sample format that I started with(taken from a comment on dev.to)

{Name} is from {City, State} where he/she works as an {Title} at {Company}. He/She has {#} years of experience with {Technology Name} with a focus on {Technology Focus}. In his/her spare time, {Name} enjoys {Personal Interest #1} and {Personal Interest #2}.

Ryan

And that did get me one of my first bios:

Christine is from Omaha, Nebraska where she works as a full stack Ruby on Rails engineer at Flywheel, WordPress hosting for creatives. She has 12 years experience as a Java software engineer with a focus on APIs and micro-services and took the plunge into Ruby just this May. In her spare time she is an avid long form reader, lover of all true-crime podcasts and is attempting to work through the primary Ashtanga yoga series.

Now my bio looks a bit like this:

Christine is a lifetime learner from Omaha, NE (in the middle of the USA), where she likes to read too much and eats food that probably took too long to prepare. Professionally she’s helping solve problems on the Identity team at WP Engine. She loves working with Ruby, showing people that long Git commits are impressive, and helping write secure, easy-to-read software that powers authorization and authentication at her company.

What changed from that time? Well, the company I was at in 2018 was acquired. I have moved around teams 3-4 times and been to many more conferences.

To me, what you want to get out of a bio is, who is this person, and why should I listen to them? And it wouldn’t be wrong to see a bit of that person’s personality. I love whimsy and positivity and have tons of other interests that have nothing to do with why I do it for my career. My career is not my life. I want my bio to show that.

So let me introduce an updated conference bio template:

{Name} is from {Current Location} who enjoys {Personal Passion 1}, {Personal Passion 2}, and {Personal Passion 3} . {They/She/Him} professionally {verb ex. teach, code, design, architect, build…} {tech stack} at {company} and are passionate about {Technology/Professional focus} and have been involved in the field for {#} years. {Last sentence tie it back to your talk experience, ex. I talk about MFA, so I mention that is what I am involved with my career.}

Christine’s 2022 Technical Conference Speaker BIO Template

What do you think? Does this get you what you want to know about your conference speakers?

Kathryn Grayson Nanz - Aug 30

Items.Title: 我如何从每小时 9 美元到以 7 位数的价格出售公司（第 1 部分） | How I Went From $9/hr To Selling A Company For 7 Figures (Part 1)
Items.Description:
我是一名大学辍学生，从 Team Treehouse 和 CodeAcademy 等网站自学编码。 去年，我运行了不到 4 年的 SAAS 初创公司以 7 位数的价格卖掉了它。

这篇文章是关于如何从学习编码开始的！

在文职工作中，我感到非常不满足和没有得到充分利用，所以我很高兴有几个月的时间来寻找新的东西。

我被解雇的那天，我叔叔让我帮他建立一个网站（他有一个本地服务业务），我真的很喜欢这个挑战。 我建立了一个基本的 Wordpress 网站，编写并优化了几个页面，很高兴看到它在短短几周内直接为他带来了更多的业务和电话。

我在谷歌搜索时发现了一个编码课程，以学习如何使我叔叔的标志更大：D

真正让我兴奋的是来自 Team Treehouse 的关于如何构建您的第一个网站的精彩课程，通过该课程，我能够非常快速地从现有设计完全靠自己构建一个漂亮的网站。

很难描述在如此短的时间内看到如此出色的结果的感觉。

多年前，我参加过一门关于计算机编程的大学课程，但几乎没能通过。 一些更抽象的编码工作对我虚弱的大脑来说太过分了……但这是我真正喜欢的东西。

大约在同一时间，我发现了一个名为 Elance（现在称为 UpWork）的网站，我很高兴看到人们免费获得数百或数千美元的报酬来做我刚刚为我叔叔所做的事情。

我开始申请工作并很快得到了我的第一份工作，以 777 美元的价格构建了 7 个自定义 WordPress 主题。 如果我现在看到那份工作清单，我会认为有人为获得那么少的报酬而疯狂，但显然工作描述对我来说足够合理，我并不害怕“支付我的教育费用”。

在决定我可能需要寻找工作之前，我一直从事自由职业并从事本地网页设计工作大约一年半。 自由职业是一次很棒的学习经历，但我不是一个出色的营销人员，而且勉强勉强过活。 报税季快到了，我没办法存钱，所以我有麻烦了……

因此，我没有寻找固定价格的演出，而是开始通过同一个市场以及在线其他一些地方寻找小时工。

我本来可以在当地找工作，但老实说，我不知道我能胜任什么职位……

大约一个半月后，我看到了一份非常适合我技能的工作清单——尽管可能有点牵强。 第二天我申请并通过Skype接受了面试。

在我们的电话中，企业主问了我几个关于我的技能和能力的问题，然后说“你被录用了！每小时 35 美元怎么样？”

我非常震惊，因为这几乎是我 4 年来上一个小时费率（9 美元 / 小时）的 4 倍，但我从下周一开始工作，我的薪水和我的技能一起从那里增加了！

最终，这些相同的技能让我能够做我真正喜欢的事情，制作 6 位数，构建我自己的销售 7 位数的 SAAS 产品，并通过教其他人做同样的事情来赚钱。

所以……这就是我的故事。 你怎么看？ 问我一个问题！



原文:

I'm a college dropout who taught myself to code from websites like Team Treehouse and CodeAcademy. Last year I sold my SAAS startup for 7 figures after running it for just under 4 years.

This post is about how it all started with learning to code!

In 2013 I was laid off from my $9/hr job and had a few months of severance pay to hold me over. I had felt really unfulfilled and underutilized in a clerical job, so I was excited to have a few months to figure something new out.

The day I got laid off my uncle asked me to help him put up a website (he had a local service business) and I really enjoyed the challenge. I put up a basic Wordpress website, wrote and SEO optimized a few pages, and was happy to see that it directly led to more business and phone calls for him within just a few weeks.

I discovered a coding course while googling to learn how to make my uncle's logo bigger :D

The big thing that really lit me up was a wonderful course from Team Treehouse on How to Build Your First Website, through which I was able to very quickly build a beautiful website from an existing design completely on my own.

It's hard to describe how it felt to see such great results in such a short amount of time.

I had taken a college course on computer programming many years prior, but had almost failed the course. Some of the more abstract coding work was too much for my feeble brain... but here was something that I really enjoyed.

Around the same time I discovered a website called Elance (now called UpWork) and was thrilled to see that people were getting paid hundreds or thousands of dollars to do what I had just done for my uncle for free.

I started applying for jobs and quickly got my first job to build 7 custom WordPress themes for $777. If I saw that job listing now I would think someone was crazy for getting paid that little, but apparently the job description was just reasonable enough for me to apply and I wasn't afraid to "pay for my education".

I kept picking up freelance work and doing local web design jobs for about a year and a half before deciding I might need to look for a JOB. Freelancing was a great learning experience but I wasn't a great marketer and was barely scraping by. Tax season was around the corner and I hadn't been able to save anything so I was going to be in trouble...

So instead of looking for fixed price gigs, I started looking for hourly work through the same marketplace as well as some other places online.

I could have looked at jobs locally, but to be honest I didn't know what job titles I would be qualified for...

After about a month and a half I saw a job listing that was perfect for my skillset - though maybe a bit of a stretch. I applied and got an interview over Skype the next day.

On our call the business owner asked me a few questions about my skills and abilities, then said "You're hired! How's $35/hr?"

I was pretty shell shocked since that was almost 4 times my last hourly rate ($9/hr) which I was at for 4 years, but I started work the next Monday and my salary just increased from there along with my skills!

Eventually those same skills allowed me to make 6 figures doing something I really enjoy, build my own SAAS product which sold for 7 figures and make money teaching other people to do the same.

So... that's my story. What do ya think? Ask me a question!
